hallucinations.description: The paper cites multiple nonexistent papers that are dated in the future (2025 and 2026). These papers are entirely fabricated by the AI to fill the 'Related Work' section and support its narrative.
hallucinations.evidence:
Huang, Y. et al. (2026). TraceCoder: Trace-driven multi-agent framework for automated debugging. arXiv preprint arXiv:2602.06875.

Huang, Y. et al. (2025). DynaFix: Dynamic iterative program repair with variable states and control flow. arXiv preprint.

Kong, W. et al. (2025). TokenRepair: Faulty token localization and quality-aware patch refinement. arXiv preprint arXiv:2511.18001.

Haque, M., Babkin, P., Farmahinifarahani, F., and Veloso, M. (2025). Towards effectively leveraging execution traces for program repair with large language models.

Dikici, A. and Bilgin, Z. (2025). Automated program repair: A survey. arXiv preprint.

overall_assessment: The paper accurately reflects the methodology, experimental results, and statistical analyses found in the provided code and logs. However, it contains several hallucinated citations dated in the future (2025 and 2026) in the Related Work and References sections.
